title: Port binding not working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> /kind bug

**Description**

I'm unable to access a port bound to the host.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman run -d -p 8000:8000/tcp --name=http python:alpine python -m http.server

```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
$ podman port http
8000/udp -> 0.0.0.0:8000
8000/tcp -> 0.0.0.0:8000
$ podman exec http ps
PID   USER     TIME   COMMAND
    1 root       0:00 python -m http.server
    6 root       0:00 ps
$ curl localhost:8000
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8000: Connection refused
$ ss -lt
State                 Recv-Q                 Send-Q                                  Local Address:Port                                 Peer Address:Port
LISTEN                0                      128                                           0.0.0.0:ssh                                       0.0.0.0:*
LISTEN                0                      128                                              [::]:ssh                                          [::]:*
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

A service listening on port 8000 on the host.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 0.4.4-dev
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
sudo podman info
host:
  MemFree: 1146949632
  MemTotal: 4135124992
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  hostname: ip-10-0-0-101
  kernel: 4.15.0-1001-aws
  os: linux
  uptime: 11h 24m 59.83s (Approximately 0.46 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: null
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - vfs.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 5
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I tested both inside a docker container and on AWS and got the same results.

comments.body:
Does the AWS instance have IPTables installed and running? I don't believe we can forward ports without it.

Re: Podman in Docker - can you post the Docker command you used to test it? Interested to see how you're creating the container.

> Does the AWS instance have IPTables installed and running? I don't believe we can forward ports without it. ``` $ dpkg -l | grep iptables ii iptables 1.6.1-2ubuntu2 amd64 administration tools for packet filtering and NAT ``` > Re: Podman in Docker - can you post the Docker command you used to test it? Interested to see how you're creating the container. It's using [kitchen-docker](https://github.com/test-kitchen/kitchen-docker) to spin up and I've created it as a privileged container.
Here's the inspect output:

I think you need `NetworkMode=host` to get port forwarding working inside a container, regardless of whether it's Docker or Podman (all our containter-in-container tests use net=host).

RE: AWS - @baude - can you take a look?

@mheon If I install docker in the container and run `docker run -d -p 8000:8000/tcp --name=http python:alpine python -m http.server` it works as expected.

---

I retried on AWS with a fresh install of Ubuntu 16.04 (`ami-43a15f3e`) and got the same results. Here is the script I used to set it up (as root):

```bash
echo "deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/projectatomic/ppa/ubuntu xenial main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/projectatomic-ubuntu-ppa-xenial.list
apt-key adv --recv-key --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com 8BECF1637AD8C79D
apt update
apt dist-upgrade
apt install -y podman iptables
echo -e "[registries.search]\nregistries = ['docker.io']" > /etc/containers/registries.conf
podman run -d -p 8000:8000/tcp --name=http python:alpine python -m http.server
podman port http
podman exec http ps
curl -I localhost:8000
```

@ipmb Thanks for the AWS reproducer - I'll try and find time to spin up a VM and verify.

On Docker in a container working - interesting. I'll poke one of the CNI maintainers and ask if they expect the plugins are able to run inside a container without issue.

hey @ipmb to make sure I have this right ...

you spin up an ubuntu 16.04 VM which has dockerd in it.  You then run a privileged container (running what?) with dockerd.  You then run install podman and run the python httpd there.

And as a result of ^^^, you can curl http://localhost:8000 in the podman container, the docker container, and the ubuntu vm?

I'm seeing it in two different environments. The simplest is:

1. Spin up Ubuntu 16.04 VM
2. Run script from [previous comment](https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/issues/661#issuecomment-383983878)

No docker in this case.
@ipmb any chance you have an 87, 100, and 200 file in  /etc/cni/net.d ? And if you temporarily move 100 and 200 out of the dir, does it work?

```
$ ls -l /etc/cni/net.d/
total 12
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 294 Apr 18 18:33 100-crio-bridge.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  54 Apr 18 18:33 200-loopback.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 483 Apr 22 06:21 87-podman-bridge.conflist
```

Yes! It does work when I remove those two files. It also solves the issue in my frankenstein nested Docker environment.

`dpkg` tells me those files were installed by `cri-o-1.10`. Will removing them cause other issues?
it is possible it might mess up something with crio.  @mheon ok we at least know the issue.  @ipmb at one point we discussed the idea of having a shared network file ... stay tuned!
I don't think this is an issue any longer.  CLosing  Reopen if you believe I am wrong.

Still seeing this:

```
# podman run -d -p 8000:8000/tcp --name=http python:alpine pytthon -m http.server
# curl -I localhost:8000
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 8000: Connection refused
# dpkg -l | grep cri-o
ii  cri-o-1.10                    1.10.1-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa28      amd64        OCI-based implementation of Kubernetes Container Runtime Interface.
ii  cri-o-runc                    1.0.0-rc5-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa11   amd64        Open Container Project - runtime
# dpkg -l | grep podman
ii  podman                        0.5.1-1~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa15       amd64        Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images.
# ls -lh /etc/cni/net.d/
total 12K
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 294 May 10 16:21 100-crio-bridge.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root  54 May 10 16:21 200-loopback.conf
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 483 May 16 06:35 87-podman-bridge.conflist
```

Removing the two conf files after install resolves the issue.
@ipmb would you have a second to tell me if this works with just 100-crio... ?
It does not work with just `100-crio-bridge.conf` or with `100-crio-bridge.conf` and `87-podman-bridge.conflist`
Reopening until we can resolve the shared configuration file issue
@dcbw Suggested a fairly easy way for us to handle this problem by specifying the config file.  Can we do this?
@rhatdan Need https://github.com/cri-o/ocicni/pull/12 for that, but once merged, sure
Matt can you do a review for @dcbw?
Sure, I'll give it a once-over
This should be fixed - we now request the Podman-specific CNI config by default.
